core_mechanism: periodic threshold-based truncation-and-renormalization of an evolving probability density, interleaved with free stochastic evolution, which acts as a stroboscopic (Poincare) map on distribution space converging to a unique fixed-point (quasi-stationary) distribution set only by the threshold and period
brief_reason: The abstract describes repeated removal of the upper/lower portion of a density above/below a threshold, driving the system to a quasi-steady state that depends only on the harvesting threshold and frequency and not on initial conditions -- the fixed point of an iterated truncate-then-evolve map.
